# Floe Language — LLM Quick Reference

Floe is a programming language that compiles to TypeScript. It targets TypeScript/React developers who want stronger guarantees. The syntax is familiar but stricter: no null, no any, no classes, no exceptions. Output is clean, readable .ts/.tsx files with zero runtime dependencies.

## Core Syntax

```floe
// Named functions use fn keyword with -> return type
fn add(a: number, b: number) -> number {
    a + b
}

// Exported function
export fn greet(name: string) -> string {
    `Hello, ${name}!`
}

// Anonymous functions use |params| body (pipe lambdas)
items |> map(|x| x + 1)

// Zero-arg lambda
onClick={|| doSomething()}

// Dot shorthand for field access in callbacks
users |> filter(.active)        // same as |u| u.active
users |> map(.name)             // same as |u| u.name
users |> filter(.id != id)      // same as |u| u.id != id

// No semicolons
const x = 5
const y = x + 1
```

## Variables

```floe
// Only const — no let, no var
const name = "Ryan"
const count = 42

// Number literal separators (underscores for readability)
const million = 1_000_000
const pi = 3.141_592
const hex = 0xFF_FF

// Type annotations
const names: Array<string> = ["a", "b", "c"]
```

## Types

```floe
// Record type
type User = {
    id: string,
    name: string,
    email: string,
}

// Union type (discriminated)
type Route =
    | Home
    | Profile(id: string)
    | Settings(tab: string)
    | NotFound

// Union types can carry data
type Validation =
    | Valid(text: string)
    | TooShort
    | TooLong
    | Empty

// Nested unions
type NetworkError =
    | Timeout(ms: number)
    | DnsFailure(host: string)

type ApiError =
    | Network(NetworkError)
    | NotFound
    | ServerError(status: number)

// String literal union (for npm interop)
type HttpMethod = "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "DELETE"

// Record type composition with spread
type BaseProps = {
    className: string,
    disabled: boolean,
}

type ButtonProps = {
    ...BaseProps,
    onClick: () -> (),
    label: string,
}
// ButtonProps has: className, disabled, onClick, label

// Multiple spreads
type A = { x: number }
type B = { y: string }
type C = { ...A, ...B, z: boolean }

// Branded types
type UserId = Brand<string, "UserId">
type Email = Brand<string, "Email">

// Opaque types (only defining module can create/read)
opaque type HashedPassword = string

// Tuples
const point: (number, number) = (10, 20)
const (x, y) = point
```

## Type Constructors and Record Spread

```floe
// Positional
const user = User("1", "Ryan", "r@test.com")

// Named arguments (any order)
const user = User(name: "Ryan", id: "1", email: "r@test.com")

// Record spread with double dot
const updated = User(..user, name: "New Name")

// Union variant construction
const err = Network(Timeout(3000))
```

## Pipe Operator

```floe
// Default: piped value becomes first argument (shorthand)
users |> filter(.active) |> map(.name) |> sort
// filter(users, .active) -> map(result, .name) -> sort(result)

const result = value
    |> multiply(2)       // multiply(value, 2)
    |> add(1)            // add(result, 1)
    |> toString          // toString(result)

// Placeholder _ for non-first position
5 |> add(3, _)              // add(3, 5) — 5 goes to second arg
42 |> wrap("[", _, "]")     // wrap("[", 42, "]") — 42 goes to second arg

// _ outside pipes creates partial application
const addTen = add(10, _)   // (x) => add(10, x)

// tap for side effects mid-pipeline
users
    |> filter(.active)
    |> tap(Console.log)    // logs value, passes it through
    |> map(.name)

// Pipe into match — pipe a value directly into pattern matching
const label = price |> match {
    _ when _ < 10 -> "cheap",
    _ when _ < 100 -> "moderate",
    _ -> "expensive",
}
// Equivalent to: match price { ... }

// Chain into match
const message = response.status
    |> match {
        200..299 -> "success",
        404 -> "not found",
        _ -> "error",
    }
```

## Match Expressions

```floe
// Match on union types (exhaustive)
match route {
    Home -> <HomePage />,
    Profile(id) -> <ProfilePage id={id} />,
    Settings(tab) -> <SettingsPage tab={tab} />,
    NotFound -> <NotFoundPage />,
}

// Match with guards
match user {
    User(age) when age > 18 -> "adult",
    User(age) -> "minor",
}

// Match on booleans (replaces if/else and ternary)
match isActive {
    true -> "active",
    false -> "inactive",
}

// Match with ranges
match status {
    200..299 -> Ok(response),
    404 -> Err(NotFound),
    _ -> Err(ServerError(status)),
}

// Match on string literal union (exhaustive)
type HttpMethod = "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "DELETE"
match method {
    "GET" -> "fetching",
    "POST" -> "creating",
    "PUT" -> "updating",
    "DELETE" -> "removing",
}

// Match with string patterns
match url {
    "/users/{id}" -> fetchUser(id),
    "/users/{id}/posts" -> fetchPosts(id),
    _ -> notFound(),
}

// Multi-depth matching
match appError {
    User(Http(Network(Timeout(ms)))) -> `Timed out after ${ms}ms`,
    Payment(CardDeclined(reason)) -> `Card declined: ${reason}`,
    _ -> showGenericError(appError),
}

// Array pattern matching
match items {
    [] -> "empty",
    [only] -> `just ${only}`,
    [first, second] -> "exactly two",
    [first, ..rest] -> `first is ${first}, rest has ${rest |> Array.length}`,
}

// Exhaustive array matching ([] + [_, ..rest] covers all cases)
match items {
    [] -> "empty",
    [head, ..tail] -> `head: ${head}`,
}

// Inline match in JSX
<div className={match active { true -> "bold", false -> "dim" }}>
    {match error {
        "" -> <span />,
        _ -> <p>{error}</p>,
    }}
</div>
```

## Result and Option

```floe
// Result<T, E> replaces exceptions
fn divide(a: number, b: number) -> Result<number, string> {
    match b {
        0 -> Err("division by zero"),
        _ -> Ok(a / b),
    }
}

// Must handle Result
match divide(10, 3) {
    Ok(value) -> Console.log(value),
    Err(msg) -> Console.error(msg),
}

// ? operator for early return
fn loadProfile(id: string) -> Result<Profile, Error> {
    const user = fetchUser(id)?        // returns Err early if it fails
    const posts = fetchPosts(user.id)?
    Ok(Profile(user, posts))
}

// Option<T> replaces null/undefined
type User = {
    name: string,
    nickname: Option<string>,
}

match user.nickname {
    Some(nick) -> nick,
    None -> user.name,
}

// Option helpers
user.nickname |> Option.unwrapOr(user.name)
user.nickname |> Option.map(|n| toUpper(n))

// Map<K, V> — immutable key-value maps
const config = Map.fromArray([("host", "localhost"), ("port", "8080")])
const updated = config |> Map.set("port", "3000")
const port = Map.get(config, "port")     // Option<string>
const keys = config |> Map.keys          // Array<string>
const merged = Map.merge(defaults, config)

// Set<T> — immutable unique collections
const tags = Set.fromArray(["urgent", "bug", "frontend"])
const updated = tags |> Set.add("backend")
const isUrgent = tags |> Set.has("urgent")    // boolean
const teamAll = Set.union(teamA, teamB)
const overlap = Set.intersect(teamA, teamB)
const diff = Set.diff(teamA, teamB)
```

## Imports

```floe
// Standard import
import { Todo, Filter } from "./types"

// npm imports are unsafe by default (might throw)
import { parseYaml } from "yaml-lib"
const result = try parseYaml(input)    // wraps in Result<T, Error>

// trusted imports skip the try requirement
import trusted { useState, useEffect } from "react"
import trusted { clsx } from "clsx"

// Per-function trust
import { trusted capitalize, fetchData } from "some-lib"
capitalize("hello")              // no try needed
const data = try fetchData()     // try required

// Importing for-block extensions
import { for User } from "./user-helpers"
import { Todo, Filter, for Array, for string } from "./todo"
```

## For Blocks (Extension Methods)

```floe
// Block form — group multiple functions under a type
for Array<Todo> {
    export fn filterBy(self, f: Filter) -> Array<Todo> {
        match f {
            All -> self,
            Active -> self |> filter(.done == false),
            Completed -> self |> filter(.done == true),
        }
    }

    export fn remaining(self) -> number {
        self |> filter(.done == false) |> length
    }
}

// Inline form — single function
export for string fn validate(self) -> Validation {
    const trimmed = self |> trim
    const len = trimmed |> String.length
    match len {
        0 -> Empty,
        1 -> TooShort,
        _ when len > 100 -> TooLong,
        _ -> Valid(trimmed),
    }
}

// Usage in pipes — self is first argument
todos |> filterBy(Filter.Active)
"hello" |> validate
```

## Traits

```floe
// Define a trait
trait Display {
    fn display(self) -> string
}

// Traits can have default implementations
trait Eq {
    fn eq(self, other: Self) -> boolean
    fn neq(self, other: Self) -> boolean {
        !(self |> eq(other))
    }
}

// Implement a trait
for User: Display {
    fn display(self) -> string {
        `${self.name} (${self.age})`
    }
}
```

## JSX / React Components

```floe
import trusted { useState } from "react"

type Todo = {
    id: string,
    text: string,
    done: boolean,
}

export fn App() -> JSX.Element {
    const [todos, setTodos] = useState<Array<Todo>>([])
    const [input, setInput] = useState("")

    fn handleAdd() {
        setTodos(todos |> append(Todo(id: "1", text: input, done: false)))
        setInput("")
    }

    <div>
        <h1>Todos</h1>
        <input
            value={input}
            onChange={|e| setInput(e.target.value)}
            onKeyDown={|e| match e.key {
                "Enter" -> handleAdd(),
                _ -> (),
            }}
        />
        <button onClick={handleAdd}>Add</button>
        <ul>
            {todos |> map(|item|
                <li key={item.id}>
                    <span className={match item.done {
                        true -> "line-through",
                        false -> "",
                    }}>
                        {item.text}
                    </span>
                    <button onClick={|| setTodos(todos |> map(|t|
                        match t.id == item.id {
                            true -> Todo(..t, done: !t.done),
                            false -> t,
                        }
                    ))}>
                        Toggle
                    </button>
                </li>
            )}
        </ul>
    </div>
}
```

## Tests

```floe
fn add(a: number, b: number) -> number { a + b }

test "addition" {
    assert add(1, 2) == 3
    assert add(-1, 1) == 0
}
```

## Special Expressions

```floe
// todo — placeholder, type never, warns at compile time
fn processPayment(order: Order) -> Result<Receipt, Error> {
    todo    // throws "not implemented" at runtime
}

// unreachable — assert impossible, type never
match key {
    "w" -> "up",
    "s" -> "down",
    _ -> unreachable,
}
```

## What's Banned

| Banned | Use Instead |
|--------|-------------|
| `any` | `unknown` + narrowing |
| `null`, `undefined` | `Option<T>` with `Some`/`None` |
| `let`, `var` | `const` |
| `class` | Functions + types |
| `enum` | `type` with `\|` variants |
| `throw` | Return `Result<T, E>` |
| `if`/`else` | `match` expression |
| `? :` (ternary) | `match` expression |
| `?.` (optional chain) | `match` on `Option` or `?` |
| `??` (nullish coalesce) | `Option.unwrapOr` |
| `=>` (arrow function) | `\|x\|` pipe lambda |
| `function` keyword | `fn` |
| `void` | Unit type `()` |
| `as` (type assertion) | Type guards or `match` |
| `return` | Implicit returns — last expression is the return value |

## Compilation Examples

| Floe | TypeScript Output |
|------|-------------------|
| `fn f(x: number) -> number { x }` | `function f(x: number): number { return x; }` |
| `a \|> f(b)` | `f(a, b)` |
| `a \|> f(b, _, c)` | `f(b, a, c)` |
| `a \|> match { 1 -> "one", _ -> "other" }` | `match a { ... }` (desugared) |
| `\|x\| x + 1` | `(x) => x + 1` |
| `.name` (in callback) | `(x) => x.name` |
| `Ok(value)` | `{ ok: true, value }` |
| `Err(error)` | `{ ok: false, error }` |
| `Some(value)` | `value` |
| `None` | `undefined` |
| `User(name: "Ry")` | `{ name: "Ry" }` |
| `User(..u, name: "New")` | `{ ...u, name: "New" }` |
| `f(name: "x", limit: 10)` | `f("x", 10)` (labels erased) |
| `Console.log(x)` | `console.log(x)` |
| `(1, 2)` | `[1, 2] as const` |
| `()` (unit) | `undefined` |

## Key Rules

1. **No semicolons** in Floe source
2. **Exported functions must have explicit return types** with `->`
3. **match is exhaustive** — all variants must be handled
4. **`?` only works** inside functions returning `Result` or `Option`
5. **`==` is structural equality** — only between same types
6. **Array index returns `Option<T>`** — must handle `None`
7. **npm imports are unsafe by default** — use `try` or mark `trusted`
8. **`self` is explicit** in for blocks — no implicit `this`
9. **No mutation** of function parameters
10. **Unused variables/imports are compile errors** (prefix with `_` to suppress)
11. **`Console.log`** not `console.log` (capital C in Floe)
12. **`Array<T>`** not `T[]` for array types
13. **Implicit returns** — last expression in a block is the return value; `return` keyword is banned
14. **Blank line before final expression** — `floe fmt` inserts a blank line before the last expression in multi-statement blocks (2+ statements) to visually separate the return value
